description: Universal learning coach eval baseline — validates teaching quality across domains, CS topics, and teaching modes

evals.name: math-quadratic-equation
evals.prompt: I want to learn the quadratic formula, middle school math level, give me a 15-minute guided walkthrough.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode A (Guided Decomposition), including: initial intuition, concrete hand-trace, key element checklist, reasoning chain, self-check list. Should NOT directly provide the formula for memorization.
assertions: routes to guided decomposition mode
includes a concrete hand-trace with specific numbers
includes key element checklist in table format
includes self-check list
does not directly give the formula for rote memorization

evals.name: socratic-economics-exam
evals.prompt: Help me prepare for my macroeconomics final exam. I know there are fiscal policy and monetary policy, but I'm fuzzy on the details.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode B (Socratic + Advanced Recall), first probe what the user already knows, then guide the user to recall and derive through a question chain — not explain directly.
assertions: routes to socratic mode
asks about the user's existing knowledge before explaining
designs a progressive question chain
includes hints about exam follow-up depth
ends with a list of weak spots

evals.name: mental-model-cell-division
evals.prompt: I want to understand the process of mitosis. It feels very abstract — can you draw a diagram to help me understand?
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode C (Mental Model + Dual Coding), including ASCII diagrams or structured visual representation alongside text explanation.
assertions: routes to mental model + dual coding mode
includes visual representation (ASCII diagram / table / flowchart)
dual explanation: text and visual
includes connection test questions
first asks about the user's existing understanding

evals.name: knowledge-source-with-doc
evals.prompt: I've uploaded a set of contract law study notes (assume provided). Help me review the difference between offer and acceptance based on these notes.
evals.expected_output: Should prioritize teaching from the user-provided notes, citing specific content from the notes — not substituting with the model's own knowledge.
assertions: explicitly references the user-provided materials
teaching content based on user materials, not model knowledge
cites knowledge source
mentions will supplement with search if materials are insufficient

evals.name: systematic-learning-french
evals.prompt: I want to systematically study the French past tense system. I have an A2 foundation. Give me a 30-minute study plan.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode F (Interleaved Practice + Generative Learning), design a mixed study plan with interleaved practice across different past tenses.
assertions: routes to interleaved practice + generative learning mode
designs a 30-minute study plan
includes mixed practice problems (interleaved across different tenses)
includes generative tasks (sentence construction, comparison, etc.)
adapts difficulty to the user's A2 level

evals.name: algorithm-two-sum
evals.prompt: 我想学习两数之和这道算法题，LeetCode 第 1 题。我有 15 分钟，希望引导式讲解。
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode A (algorithm concretization), read domain-cs context, include intuition, hand-trace, variable table, code mapping, self-check. Should NOT give complete solution code upfront.
assertions: routes to guided decomposition / algorithm concretization mode
includes hand-trace with a small concrete example
includes variable or key element checklist in table format
includes self-check list
does not give complete solution code upfront
applies CS teaching patterns consistent with domain-cs

evals.name: socratic-redis-interview
evals.prompt: 帮我准备 Redis 持久化的面试，我知道有 RDB 和 AOF 两种，但细节记不清了。
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode B, probe existing knowledge first, use question chain, include interviewer follow-up depth, end with weak spots — not lecture directly.
assertions: routes to socratic mode
asks about existing knowledge before explaining
designs progressive question chain
includes interviewer follow-up depth hints
ends with weak spot summary

evals.name: mental-model-transformer
evals.prompt: 我想理解 Transformer 的自注意力机制，感觉很抽象，能画个图帮我理解吗？
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode C with ASCII or structured visual plus text; ask about prior understanding first.
assertions: routes to mental model + dual coding mode
includes visual representation (ASCII diagram / table / flowchart)
dual text and visual explanation
includes connection test questions
asks about user's existing understanding first
